title: Hypotheses, Riskiest Assumptions, and Disposable Prototypes · Product Evidence Studio
summary: A guided product team extends ranked assumption map and prototype evidence while preserving every earlier problem, accessibility, security, testing, operations, and recovery requirement. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of ranked assumption map and prototype evidence while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Express desirable, usable, feasible, viable, ethical, accessible, secure, and operable assumptions as falsifiable claims.
Prioritize assumptions by uncertainty, consequence, dependency, and cheapest discriminating evidence.
Choose paper, clickable, coded, data, integration, or operational prototype fidelity to answer one question without production overinvestment.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Assumption Register
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A polished coded prototype appears persuasive, but it tests no predeclared assumption and quietly becomes production code after one positive comment. The workshop variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Assumption Register.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for express desirable, usable, feasible, viable, ethical, accessible, secure, and operable assumptions as falsifiable claims. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Assumption Register, keep Assumption Register connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Prototype Disposal
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Prototype Disposal: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A polished coded prototype appears persuasive, but it tests no predeclared assumption and quietly becomes production code after one positive comment. The workshop variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate disposable exploration from production code and record what can and cannot transfer after the experiment.
